card.intuition: A release strategy where you roll out a new feature to a tiny subset of users first (the 'canaries') before rolling it out to the whole population.
card.analogy: A canary in a coal mine: miners used to take a bird into the mine. If there was dangerous gas, the bird would get sick first, warning the miners to get out. In software, if 1% of users get errors, you stop the release before 100% of users are affected.
when_to_use: Testing new, complex code under real-world traffic patterns
Gathering feedback from a small group of beta testers
Ensuring that new features don't cause performance regressions
gotchas: Complexity: you must be able to route traffic based on user headers or percentages, which requires an advanced load balancer or service mesh.
Monitoring: a canary release is useless if you aren't watching the error rates specifically for the canary group.

exercise.prompt: How does a Canary Release differ from a Blue-Green deployment?
